document_id: Ombudsman Listed by Area Agency on Aging

findings.description: The title block layout and visual hierarchy are significantly altered.
findings.evidence: In the PDF, the title consists of three centered lines with consistent importance. In the HTML, the first two lines ('Division of Aging', 'North Carolina...') are rendered as small, left-aligned text at the very top, while the third line ('Office of the State...') becomes a large, prominent heading with a bottom border.

findings.description: Footer text is inserted out of visual reading sequence near the top of the document.
findings.evidence: The text 'As of February 2026', which appears as a bottom footer in the PDF, is placed near the top of the HTML document, before the main heading.

findings.description: Right-alignment of phone numbers in the contact section is lost.
findings.evidence: In the PDF, phone numbers for the ombudsman contacts are formatted to be flush right. In the HTML, they are inline and directly follow the email addresses, losing the aligned layout.

findings.description: Hyperlinks from the source PDF were converted to plain text rather than active anchor tags (<a>).
findings.evidence: The PDF contains blue, underlined text for emails and URLs (e.g., 'https://www.ncdhhs.gov/aging' and 'renee.kea@dhhs.nc.gov'). In the HTML, these are rendered as plain text (e.g., 'Website: https://www.ncdhhs.gov/aging' and 'renee.kea@dhhs.nc.gov') without the corresponding <a href="..."> markup.
